appearance_show_stopped_desc: Display stopped and exited containers in lists
appearance_highlight_updates_desc: Highlight container rows in amber when updates are available
appearance_show_changelog_links_desc: Show a release-notes icon next to images with updates available
appearance_show_changelog_tooltip: Surface a release-notes link next to the image name on rows with updates available. The link is resolved from the image's org.opencontainers.image.source label, from the ghcr.io registry path, or from an explicit dockhand.changelog.url label override.
appearance_compact_ports_desc: Show first port with +N count instead of all ports
appearance_show_exposed_ports_desc: Display internal container ports in the container list grid
appearance_show_exposed_ports_tooltip: Shows internal container ports (from EXPOSE directives) that are not published to the host. These appear in the container list with an amber badge to distinguish them from published port mappings.
appearance_honor_proxy_labels_desc: Show URLs inferred from Traefik and Pangolin labels alongside dockhand.url
appearance_honor_proxy_labels_tooltip: Parse traefik.http.routers.<name>.rule, pangolin.public-resources.<name>.full-domain, and pangolin.private-resources.<name>.full-domain labels and surface the resulting URLs as clickable pills next to ports. When off, only explicit dockhand.url labels are shown.
appearance_date_format_desc: How dates are displayed throughout the app
appearance_animate_icons_desc: Spinners during pulls, scans and updates.
appearance_theme_profile_hint: Personal theme preferences can be configured in your {link}.
appearance_theme_global_hint: Theme and font settings are global when authentication is disabled.
confirmations_destructive_desc: Show confirmation dialogs before deleting resources
login_subtitle: Sign in to your Dockhand account
settings_general_default_timezone_desc: Default timezone for new environments. Used for scheduled tasks like auto-updates.
settings_general_log_buffer_size_desc: Maximum number of log lines kept per container panel. Older lines are dropped when the limit is exceeded.
settings_general_download_format_desc: Format when downloading files from containers or volumes. "No archive" emits raw bytes for single files; directories still download as tar.
settings_general_format_log_timestamps_d: Convert ISO timestamps in logs to your configured date/time format
settings_general_format_log_timestamps_i: Docker logs use UTC timestamps by default. When enabled, timestamps like {example} are converted to local time using your date/time settings.
settings_general_compose_template_desc: Default YAML content when creating a new stack.
settings_general_grype_image_desc: Docker image for Grype scanner. Pin to a specific version for supply chain security.
settings_general_trivy_image_desc: Docker image for Trivy scanner. Pin to a specific version for supply chain security.
settings_general_grype_args_desc: Use {placeholder} as placeholder for the image name
settings_general_trivy_args_desc: Use {placeholder} as placeholder for the image name
settings_general_scanner_cache_desc: Remove cached vulnerability databases to free disk space. Next scan will re-download fresh data (~200MB).
settings_general_event_collection_mode_t: Stream: Continuous event stream from Docker, instant notifications, higher CPU usage. Poll: Periodic checks for new events, slight notification delay, lower CPU usage.
settings_general_metrics_interval_toolti: How often to collect CPU/memory metrics from running containers. Lower intervals provide more frequent updates but increase CPU usage.
settings_general_schedule_cleanup_desc: Delete executions older than specified days
settings_general_event_cleanup_desc: Delete events older than specified days
settings_general_volume_helper_cleanup_d: Automatically removes temporary containers used for browsing volume contents. Runs every 30 minutes and on startup.
settings_general_scanner_cleanup_desc: Remove cached vulnerability databases to reclaim disk space
settings_general_protect_scanner_images_: Skip grype and trivy images during "Prune all unused"

When ON, "Prune all unused" skips Dockhand's grype and trivy scanner images so the next scan doesn't have to re-pull them (and re-download the ~100MB vuln database). When OFF, prune behaves like vanilla Docker and may remove them.
settings_general_label_filter_matching_t: Controls how multiple selected labels filter environments on the dashboard. "Any": shows environments that have at least one of the selected labels. "All": shows only environments that have every selected label.
settings_env_activity_collect_desc: Track container events (start, stop, restart, etc.) from this environment in real-time
settings_env_activity_metrics_desc: Collect CPU and memory usage statistics from this environment
settings_env_activity_highlight_desc: Show amber glow when container values change in the containers list
settings_env_activity_disk_warning_desc: Send notifications when Docker disk usage exceeds the threshold
settings_env_updates_check_desc: Periodically check all containers in this environment for available image updates.
settings_env_updates_check_enable_desc: Automatically check for container updates on a schedule
settings_env_updates_auto_update_desc: When enabled, containers will be updated automatically when new images are found. When disabled, only sends notifications about available updates.
settings_env_updates_block_vuln_desc: Block auto-updates if the new image has vulnerabilities exceeding this criteria
settings_env_updates_info_scan: New images are pulled to a temporary tag, scanned, then deployed if they pass the vulnerability check. Blocked images are deleted automatically.
settings_env_updates_info_auto: Containers will be updated automatically when new images are available.
settings_env_updates_info_notify: You'll receive notifications when updates are available. Containers won't be modified.
settings_env_updates_prune_desc: Automatically remove unused Docker images on a schedule to free up disk space.
settings_env_updates_prune_enable_desc: Automatically remove unused images on a schedule
settings_env_updates_prune_dangling_desc: Only removes untagged image layers (safest option)
settings_env_updates_prune_all_desc: Removes all images not used by any container (more aggressive)
settings_env_updates_prune_info: Images in use by running or stopped containers will never be removed.
settings_env_updates_timezone_desc: Used for scheduling auto-updates, git syncs, and image pruning
settings_env_event_container_unhealthy_d: When a container health check fails
settings_env_event_container_healthy_des: When a container health check recovers
settings_env_event_container_oom_desc: When a container is killed due to out of memory
settings_env_event_container_updated_des: When a container image is updated
settings_env_event_auto_update_success_d: Container successfully updated to new image
settings_env_event_auto_update_blocked_d: Update blocked due to vulnerability criteria
settings_env_event_git_sync_success_desc: Git stack synced and deployed successfully
settings_env_event_git_sync_failed_desc: Git stack sync or deploy failed
settings_env_event_vulnerability_critica: Critical vulnerabilities found in image scan
settings_env_event_image_pulled_desc: When a new image is pulled
settings_env_delete_intro_post: and the following directories will be permanently removed from the Dockhand host:
settings_env_delete_unknown: Couldn't list the stacks on this environment — proceed only if you're sure what's deployed here.
settings_env_delete_no_stacks: No stacks are currently tracked on this environment.
settings_env_delete_removed_suffix: tracked here will be removed from Dockhand's database.
settings_env_delete_running_pre: Running containers on the Docker/Hawser host are
settings_env_delete_running_post: stopped. You can stop or remove them separately.
settings_env_modal_conn_socket_desc: Connect via Docker socket on the same machine. Default path: /var/run/docker.sock. Also works with Docker Desktop and OrbStack.
settings_env_modal_conn_direct_desc: Connect directly to Docker Engine API. Requires Docker to expose its API on a TCP port (default 2375/2376). Best for LAN environments.
settings_env_modal_conn_hawser_standard_: Hawser agent listens on a port and Dockhand connects to it. Good for LAN with static IPs.
settings_env_modal_conn_hawser_edge_desc: Hawser agent initiates outbound WebSocket to Dockhand. No port forwarding needed. Perfect for VPS, NAT, or dynamic IPs.
settings_env_modal_conn_socket_short: Connect via Unix socket on the same machine.
settings_env_modal_conn_direct_short: Connect directly to Docker Engine API on TCP port.
settings_env_modal_conn_hawser_edge_shor: agent connects out to Dockhand. No port forwarding needed.
settings_env_modal_tls_desc: TLS certificates for mTLS authentication (RSA or ECDSA). Paste the PEM content or upload a file.
settings_env_modal_tls_ca_self_desc: Paste the CA certificate or upload a file if agent uses self-signed TLS (RSA or ECDSA).
settings_env_modal_token_copy_warn: Copy this token now — it won't be shown again!
settings_env_modal_token_manual_pre: Enter a token manually or generate one. Set the same token as
settings_env_modal_token_manual_post: env var on your Hawser agent.
settings_env_modal_run_agent: Run Hawser agent on the target host:
settings_env_modal_gen_token_hint: Generate a token now. It will be saved when you add the environment.
settings_env_modal_token_copy_warn2: Copy this token now - you'll need it for the Hawser agent!
settings_env_modal_token_save_warn: Save this token now - it won't be shown again!
settings_env_modal_no_token: No token generated yet. Click Generate above.
settings_env_modal_public_ip_tip: IP address or hostname where container ports are accessible from your browser. For local Docker, use the server's LAN IP.
settings_env_modal_public_ip_desc: Used for clickable port links on the containers page
settings_env_modal_enable_scan_desc: Scan images for known security vulnerabilities
settings_env_modal_scanner_info: Scanner images will be pulled automatically on first scan. Vulnerability databases are cached in Docker volumes for faster subsequent scans.
settings_env_modal_notif_select: Select which notification channels should send alerts for events from this environment.
settings_env_modal_notif_none_hint: Create notification channels in the Notifications settings tab first.
settings_env_modal_notif_configure: Configure which notification channels should send alerts for events from this environment.
settings_env_modal_notif_activity_auto: Activity collection will be enabled automatically when you add a channel.
settings_env_modal_notif_add_hint: Add a channel below to receive alerts for this environment
settings_env_modal_no_channels_pre: No notification channels have been created yet.
settings_env_modal_no_channels_admin: No notification channels have been created yet. Contact your administrator to configure notification channels.
settings_env_modal_rename_intro: The following directories will be moved on the Dockhand host:
settings_env_modal_rename_unknown: Couldn't list the stacks on this environment — proceed only if you're sure what's deployed here.
settings_env_modal_rename_unknown_strong: Any existing stacks will need to be redeployed after the rename
settings_env_modal_rename_unknown_post: or their next container restart will fail.
settings_env_modal_rename_safe: No stacks are currently deployed on this environment, so the rename is safe.
settings_env_modal_rename_redeploy_suffi: on this environment will need to be redeployed after the rename.
settings_env_modal_rename_redeploy_note: Running containers will keep working, but their compose project labels still reference the old path. Without a redeploy, the next container restart will fail because the old path no longer exists.
settings_env_modal_rename_hawser_note: Their containers run on the Hawser agent host, where the deploy directory doesn't include the env name — those keep working without a redeploy. Only the local editor source and git clone caches move.
settings_env_modal_pem_invalid: {file} does not look like a PEM file (no BEGIN block)
settings_env_modal_test_edge: Edge mode - connection will be tested when agent connects
settings_env_modal_err_host_invalid: Enter an IP address or hostname only (no protocol or port)
